definition: A sentence worded or expressed so as to elicit information
example: To be, or not to be, that is the question: Whether 'tis nobler in the mind to suffer the slings and arrows of outrageous fortune, or to take arms against a sea of troubles, and by opposing end them.
speech: Hamlet, Act 3 Scene 1 (Soliloquy)

definition: Having or showing fine personal qualities or high moral principles
example: To be, or not to be, that is the question: Whether 'tis nobler in the mind to suffer the slings and arrows of outrageous fortune, or to take arms against a sea of troubles, and by opposing end them.
speech: Hamlet, Act 3 Scene 1 (Soliloquy)

definition: Chance or luck as an external, arbitrary force affecting human affairs
example: To be, or not to be, that is the question: Whether 'tis nobler in the mind to suffer the slings and arrows of outrageous fortune, or to take arms against a sea of troubles, and by opposing end them.
speech: Hamlet, Act 3 Scene 1 (Soliloquy)

definition: Fluent or persuasive in speaking or writing
example: What a piece of work is a man! How noble in reason, how infinite in faculty! In form and moving how express and admirable! In action how like an angel! In apprehension how like a god!
speech: Hamlet, Act 2 Scene 2

definition: Limitless or endless in space, extent, or size
example: What a piece of work is a man! How noble in reason, how infinite in faculty! In form and moving how express and admirable! In action how like an angel! In apprehension how like a god!
speech: Hamlet, Act 2 Scene 2

definition: A gap in a wall, barrier, or defense
example: Once more unto the breach, dear friends, once more; or close the wall up with our English dead.
speech: Henry V, Act 3 Scene 1

definition: A man or boy in relation to other sons and daughters of his parents
example: We few, we happy few, we band of brothers; for he today that sheds his blood with me shall be my brother.
speech: Henry V, Act 4 Scene 3 (St Crispin's Day Speech)

definition: High respect; great esteem
example: We few, we happy few, we band of brothers; for he today that sheds his blood with me shall be my brother.
speech: Henry V, Act 4 Scene 3 (St Crispin's Day Speech)

definition: A person from the same country as someone else
example: Friends, Romans, countrymen, lend me your ears; I come to bury Caesar, not to praise him.
speech: Julius Caesar, Act 3 Scene 2 (Mark Antony's Funeral Oration)

definition: Profoundly immoral and wicked
example: The evil that men do lives after them; the good is oft interred with their bones.
speech: Julius Caesar, Act 3 Scene 2 (Mark Antony's Funeral Oration)

definition: Place (a corpse) in a grave or tomb
example: The evil that men do lives after them; the good is oft interred with their bones.
speech: Julius Caesar, Act 3 Scene 2 (Mark Antony's Funeral Oration)

definition: A person who is skilled in public speaking
example: I am no orator, as Brutus is; but, as you know me all, a plain blunt man, that love my friend.
speech: Julius Caesar, Act 3 Scene 2 (Mark Antony's Funeral Oration)

definition: A raised floor or platform, typically in a theater, on which actors, entertainers, or speakers perform
example: All the world's a stage, and all the men and women merely players; they have their exits and their entrances; and one man in his time plays many parts.
speech: As You Like It, Act 2 Scene 7 (Jaques)

definition: A person taking part in a sport or game
example: All the world's a stage, and all the men and women merely players; they have their exits and their entrances; and one man in his time plays many parts.
speech: As You Like It, Act 2 Scene 7 (Jaques)

definition: Dissatisfaction with one's circumstances; lack of contentment
example: Now is the winter of our discontent made glorious summer by this sun of York.
speech: Richard III, Act 1 Scene 1 (Opening soliloquy)

definition: A country, state, or territory ruled by a king or queen
example: A horse! a horse! my kingdom for a horse!
speech: Richard III, Act 5 Scene 4

definition: A cylinder or block of wax or tallow with a central wick that is lit to produce light as it burns
example: Out, out, brief candle! Life's but a walking shadow, a poor player that struts and frets his hour upon the stage and then is heard no more.
speech: Macbeth, Act 5 Scene 5 (Tomorrow and tomorrow and tomorrow soliloquy)

definition: A dark area or shape produced by a body coming between rays of light and a surface
example: Out, out, brief candle! Life's but a walking shadow, a poor player that struts and frets his hour upon the stage and then is heard no more.
speech: Macbeth, Act 5 Scene 5 (Tomorrow and tomorrow and tomorrow soliloquy)

definition: A fixed luminous point in the night sky that is a large, remote incandescent body like the sun
example: The fault, dear Brutus, is not in our stars, but in ourselves, that we are underlings.
speech: Julius Caesar, Act 1 Scene 2 (Cassius to Brutus)

definition: Possessing or showing courage or determination
example: Cowards die many times before their deaths; the valiant never taste of death but once.
speech: Julius Caesar, Act 2 Scene 2 (Caesar to Calpurnia)
